Halsey's remnant rally and charge (Rodes concurs); the 12th + 53d NC (Daniel's) 'first to reach the railroad along which the enemy were retreating', prisoners 'numberless... cut off'; the remnant attached to Ramseur ('I raised no question of rank'); July 2 night advance 'to certain destruction' and his own retreat order; July 3 in the road all day; 'fell back about 3 a.m. of July 4.' Second report: the July 4-7 train escort (cut train recovered at the turnpike by dawn; the Hagerstown ambuscade 'killed, wounded, and captured as many of the enemy as I had men', loss 3 k / 6 w; Williamsport provost duty, 'when my connection with the brigade ceased' - the quiet relief-of-command pin). Return p. 342: brigade 820 aggregate.

clockProfile.assessment: No July 1-3 clock (first usable stated time is 3 a.m. July 4 withdrawal); sequence-only; kind-none with reason per convention.

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. S. D. Ramseur, Ramseur's Brigade, Rodes's Division (OR 27/2 pp. 587-588, No. 518)
sources.author: Stephen D. Ramseur
sources.editionNote: Fetch-verified 2026-07-12 (civilwar.com p. 587 + IA full text pp. 587-588 in full). Dated July 30. July 1: 'in rear of the division train, as a guard on the march from Heidlersburg' - arrived 'after the division had formed line of battle'; held in reserve to support Doles/O'Neal/Iverson 'according to circumstances'; 'After resting about fifteen minutes', the split order (2d + 4th NC to O'Neal, 14th + 30th to Iverson); 'I found three regiments of Iverson's command almost annihilated, and the Third Alabama Regiment coming out of the fight from Iverson's right' - the ALMOST-ANNIHILATED reading from the rescuing commander; Battle's 3d Ala self-attached ('he came in at the right place, at the right time, and in the right way'); THE COUNTER-STROKE: 'With these regiments... I turned the enemy's strong position in a body of woods, surrounded by a stone fence, by attacking en masse on his right flank, driving him back, and getting in his rear'; the 12th NC front push; 'the enemy... ran off the field in confusion, leaving his killed and wounded and between 800 and 900 prisoners in our hands. The enemy was pushed through Gettysburg to the heights beyond, when I received an order to halt, and form line of battle in a street in Gettysburg running east and west.' Lt. F. M. Harney (14th NC sharpshooters) 'whipped a Yankee regiment (One hundred and fiftieth Pennsylvania)... and took their regimental colors from them with his own hands' (both-sided with the pass-4 Stone dossier; Rodes adds Harney mw). July 2 evening (the pass-8/9 frame's missing piece): at dark ordered to move right till Doles cleared the town then advance on Cemetery Hill, told 'the remaining brigades of the division would be governed by my movements'; 'Obeyed this order until within 200 yards of the enemy's position, where batteries were discovered in position to pour upon our lines direct, cross, and enfilade fires. Two lines of infantry behind stone walls and breastworks were supporting these batteries' - THE RECONNAISSANCE THAT STOPPED THE DIVISION (Rodes: 'certain important facts... as to the nature of the ground'); withdrew to 'a deep road some 300 yards in rear... in readiness to attack at daylight.' July 3: skirmishing all day 'exposed to the artillery of the enemy and our own short-range guns, by the careless use or imperfect ammunition of which I lost 7 men killed and wounded' (FRIENDLY-FIRE EC6 row). Casualty table by regiment: 2d NC 32, 4th NC 56, 14th NC 44, 30th NC 45 = 177 - REPORT=RETURN EXACT AT ALL FOUR REGIMENTS (p. 342: 4+27+1 / 8+24+24 / 5+37+2 / 6+34+5).

clockProfile.assessment: No stated clock (dark/daylight lighting terms only); the fifteen-minute rest is a duration; kind-none with reason.

sources.title: Report of Brig. Gen. J. B. Gordon, Gordon's Brigade, Early's Division (OR 27/2 pp. 491-493, No. 477)
sources.author: John B. Gordon
sources.editionNote: Fetch-verified 2026-07-12 (civilwar.com pp. 492-493 + IA full text pp. 491-493 in full). Campaign report (Winchester, York/Wrightsville - the bridge fire and his fire-line defense of the town - then Gettysburg). July 1: formed 'on the right of the division, one regiment (the Twenty-sixth Georgia) having been detached to support the artillery under Lieutenant-Colonel Jones' - EC2 DETACHMENT PRIMARY; 'About 3 p.m. I was ordered to move my brigade forward to the support of Major-General Rodes' left' - THE CA-J1P-3 EXECUTOR ORDER CLOCK (pairs with Schurz's 'about 3 o'clock' receiving clock across the lines; upgrades the skeleton's 'Early's OR about 3 o'clock (MEMORY-grade, fetch)' precondition to a page-verified brigade primary); the deliberate slow approach ('men were much fatigued... I therefore caused them to move forward slowly until within about 300 yards'); THE KNOLL FIGHT: Doles's flank event recorded ('The enemy had succeeded in gaining a position upon the left flank of Doles' brigade'), the covering second line 'drawn up in a strong position on the crest of a hill'; THE MOVEMENT-LEG OBSTACLE PRIMARY: 'Moving forward under heavy fire over rail and plank fences, and crossing a creek whose banks were so abrupt as to prevent a passage excepting at certain points'; 'the colors on portions of the two lines were separated by a space of less than 50 paces, when his line was broken'; the failed change-of-front, the second line driven 'in the greatest confusion'; 'Among the latter was a division commander (General [F. C.] Barlow), who was severely wounded. I was here ordered by Major-General Early to halt' - the Barlow pin + the halt order BOTH in the executor primary. EC6/EC2: 'I carried into action about 1,200 men, one regiment having been detached'; 'The loss of the brigade in killed and wounded was 350, of whom 40 were killed'; the enemy's loss read ('nearly 300 of whom were buried on the ground where my brigade fought'); 'the division inspector credits this brigade with about 1,800 [prisoners]' - carried as stated (inspector-credit class, not adopted as count). July 2-3 THE DISMISSAL SENTENCE: 'The movements during the succeeding days of the battle (July 2 and 3), I do not consider of sufficient importance to mention' - first-class negative evidence for the brigade's July 2 evening non-commitment (the pass-8 ECH record's Gordon-did-not-advance side, attested by silence FROM THE COMMANDER HIMSELF); July 5 Fairfield rear-guard (26th Ga deployed, 8-10 k&w); recrossed at Williamsport July 14. Return p. 341: 380 aggregate (71/270/39) vs his 350 k&w - both carried.

clockProfile.assessment: One clock: 'About 3 p.m.' movement order agrees with Schurz's receiving 'about 3 o'clock' and sits inside CA-J1P-3's 15:00-15:30 adoption; single statement, class default envelope.
